assumptions: The intragroup dividend has given rise to a recognised foreign-currency receivable before settlement.
The question concerns the accounting in consolidated financial statements under IFRS.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge accounting as a distinct hedge accounting model, including for foreign currency risk on highly probable forecast transactions and certain recognised items.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge accounting as a separate hedge accounting model for exposure to changes in fair value of recognised assets or liabilities attributable to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 treat a hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a distinct hedge accounting model for foreign currency exposure in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Foreign currency accounting under IAS 21
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 21 provides the non-hedge accounting model for exchange differences on foreign-currency monetary items, including intragroup monetary balances in consolidated financial statements.
